project.title: Big Data & Machine Learning -- Clustering Analysis
project.description: Interactive Tableau dashboards for clustering experiments conducted using PySpark MLlib and scikit-learn on the Bag-of-Words (KOS) dataset.

dashboards.name: Data Quality & EDA
dashboards.purpose: Analyze document structure, distribution characteristics, and lexical complexity before model training.
dashboards.key_insight: Long-tail distributions and strong correlation between document length and vocabulary growth.

dashboards.name: Model Performance Comparison
dashboards.key_insight: Models with high silhouette and low Davies-Bouldin index demonstrate superior clustering quality.

dashboards.name: Clustering Structure

calculated_field.name: Selected Cluster
calculated_field.logic: CASE [Select Algorithm] WHEN 'KMeans' THEN [km_cluster] WHEN 'BisectingKMeans' THEN [bkm_cluster] WHEN 'GMM' THEN [gmm_cluster] END

dashboards.key_insight: Cluster separation and distribution vary across algorithms, highlighting modeling differences.

dashboards.name: Scalability & Performance
dashboards.key_insight: Training time trends demonstrate distributed scalability and cost-performance trade-offs.

data_processing_workflow: Data ingestion via Spark from Bag-of-Words dataset
Feature engineering using TF-IDF and PCA

steps: Open Tableau workbook and refresh data sources

conclusion: Complete analytical visualization layer for distributed clustering experiments supporting interpretability, performance comparison, structural validation, and scalability evaluation.
